hero.subtitle: Award-winning landscape architecture spanning 14 years across China, Hong Kong, and beyond.

footer.description: Award-winning landscape architecture studio with offices in Shenzhen and Hong Kong. We create living environments that resonate with the human spirit.
footer.studioDescription: Award-winning landscape architecture studio with offices in Shenzhen and Hong Kong. We create living environments that resonate with the human spirit.

ai.title: Design Consultant
ai.welcome: Hello. I am the EDGE Landscape Architecture design consultant. How can I assist with your landscape architecture needs today?
ai.error: I apologize, but I'm currently unable to connect to my design knowledge base. Please try again later.
ai.disclaimer: AI can make mistakes. Please verify important information.

1.title: Shenzhen Xili Railway Station Hub Silk Park
1.subtitle: Sustainable urban park integrating high-speed railway infrastructure
1.description: Sustainable urban park design integrating high-speed railway infrastructure with public green spaces and water management systems. Collaborated with Nikken Sekkei as architectural consultant. Client: China 4th Railway Civil Engineering Group. Project value: $42.8 million USD.
keyResponsibilities: Project management from conceptual design to construction documentation
Design leadership coordinating internal teams and external consultants
Weekly meetings with architectural consultants to align design documents
Team collaboration, mentorship of junior staff, and AI-assisted computational design workflows

2.title: Nanshan Innovation Boulevard
2.subtitle: Innovation district connectivity through sustainable transportation
2.description: Comprehensive 8-kilometer boulevard landscape design promoting innovation district connectivity and sustainable transportation. Client: SUTPC (Shenzhen Urban Transport Planning Center). Project value: $85.71 million USD.
keyResponsibilities: Design schedule creation and weekly client meetings
Contract and client management
Quality control through site inspections
Sustainability planning and environmental considerations

3.title: Baguang Coastal Landscape Design
3.subtitle: Resilient coastal design addressing climate change
3.description: Resilient coastal design spanning 107 hectares, addressing climate change impacts while creating diverse public spaces and ecological restoration. Client: Shenzhen Water and Planning Design Group. Project value: $57.2 million USD.
keyResponsibilities: Multi-disciplinary team coordination (ecologists, hydrologists, planners)
Diverse public space design for parks, plazas, streetscapes
City resilience design integration
Stakeholder engagement with communities and government agencies

4.title: Dongguan Humen TOD Railway Station
4.subtitle: Award-winning TOD development integrating landscape with rail
4.description: Award-winning TOD development integrating landscape design with high-speed rail infrastructure. Client: Poly Real Estate Group. Winner of the 2021 Yuanye Award Gold Prize. Project value: $2.9 million USD.
keyResponsibilities: Project bidding and creative design concept development
Site survey organization and condition assessment
Design sketches from concept to construction stages
Monthly site inspection reports and budget management

5.title: Avenue of Stars, Tsim Sha Tsui
5.subtitle: Iconic waterfront promenade renovation
5.description: Iconic waterfront promenade renovation combining cultural heritage with modern landscape design. Concept design by James Corner Field Operations; construction documentation by URBIS. Client: New World Property. Project value: $10 million USD.
keyResponsibilities: Construction documentation specialist in collaboration with James Corner Field Operations
Design development and technical coordination with lighting, structure, and drainage consultants
Site supervision, mock-up component review, and implementation oversight
Cultural heritage integration, materials selection, and quality control

6.title: Yantai Bajiao Bay International Convention Center
6.subtitle: Large-scale convention center landscape design
6.description: Large-scale convention center landscape design integrating "City Shore Cloud Waves & Sea Cloud Shell" concept. 8 indoor exhibition halls covering 56,000m². Project investment: $465 million USD.
keyResponsibilities: Project Manager overseeing full design and construction lifecycle
Concept design and design development
Client presentation leadership for design concepts and scheme proposals
Creative scheme development and design iteration management

7.title: Yuen Long South Residential Development
7.subtitle: Environmental assessment and landscape design strategy
7.description: Environmental assessment and landscape design strategy for large-scale 120-hectare residential development planning. Completed at ARUP Hong Kong for the Hong Kong Planning Department.
keyResponsibilities: Environmental assessment identifying valuable landscape resources and visual impact
Landscape design strategy including tree preservation, streetscape, and green infrastructure
Urban planting design guideline development with species specification
Multi-disciplinary coordination with ecologists, engineers, and architects at ARUP

8.title: Mountain and Blue Residential Project
8.subtitle: Remarkable landscape design capitalizing on terrain
8.description: Remarkable landscape design by New World Group capitalizing on "three-sided mountain, one-sided sea" terrain. Features a 24-meter three-tiered waterfall, 360-meter spring water system, and terraced design.
keyResponsibilities: Terrain elevation management through ingenious terrace design
Super high-rise setback and overhang design mimicking mountain shapes
24-meter three-tiered waterfall and spring water system design
Rock-wall waterfalls and native plant ecological buffer creation

9.title: Yuexiu Nanzhou Road Residential Garden
9.subtitle: Premium residential landscape with integrated water systems
9.description: Premium residential landscape featuring integrated water systems, modern architectural elements, and sustainable urban design with innovative circular building forms and terraced landscaping.
keyResponsibilities: Cross-disciplinary team coordination between architects, engineers, and landscape consultants
Project timeline management and milestone tracking
Quality assurance and design compliance monitoring throughout construction
Budget coordination and resource allocation management

0.name: Director Zhang Wei
0.role: China 4th Railway Civil Engineering Group — Shenzhen Xili Railway Station Hub
0.quote: EDGE's integration of park design with our high-speed rail infrastructure was exceptional. The Silk Park concept transformed what could have been a utilitarian transit hub into a genuine public amenity.

1.name: Chief Planner Li Ming
1.quote: The 8-kilometer boulevard design exceeded our expectations in creating a cohesive identity for the innovation district. Their sustainable transportation planning set a new benchmark for Shenzhen.

2.name: Project Director Chen Hao
2.role: Poly Real Estate Group — Dongguan Humen TOD Station
2.quote: Winning the 2021 Yuanye Award Gold Prize validated what we knew from day one — EDGE brought a level of design ambition to our TOD project that was truly award-worthy.

about.name: Weihao DU
about.title: Associate Landscape Architect
about.summary: Internationally experienced Landscape Designer with 14 years of diverse projects including large-scale city planning, waterfront and civic parks, high-end commercial complexes, and residential landscapes across mainland China, Hong Kong, Taiwan, and Melbourne.
about.aiSummary: Proficient in leveraging cutting-edge AI tools including Claude Code, Cursor, Replit, ChatGPT, Midjourney, and CapCut to enhance design workflows, rapid prototyping, and digital content creation — bridging traditional landscape architecture with emerging creative technology.
about.certification: Guangdong Province Intermediate Landscape Architect, China

people.headline: A hybrid studio of human vision and digital agents
people.description: EDGE operates as a future-forward practice where human creativity directs a team of specialized AI agents — each trained in a core discipline of landscape architecture, from planning to visualization.

weihao.name: Ryonx DU
weihao.bio: 14 years of international landscape architecture experience across China, Hong Kong, and Melbourne. Former Associate at AECOM. Bridging traditional design practice with AI-driven creative workflows.

novaLin.name: Nova.Lin
novaLin.bio: Specializes in large-scale master planning and land-use strategy. Trained on regional ecological frameworks, greenway networks, and policy-responsive site analysis for projects spanning 50–500 hectares.

axiomK.name: Axiom.K
axiomK.bio: Focused on detail design and planting composition. Generates grading plans, hardscape detailing, and species-specific planting palettes calibrated to subtropical and temperate climatic zones.

terraZhang.name: Terra.Zhang
terraZhang.bio: Coordinates multi-disciplinary workflows across architecture, civil engineering, and landscape consultants. Manages project timelines, deliverable tracking, and client communication protocols.

vectorWu.name: Vector.Wu
vectorWu.bio: Produces presentation layouts, competition boards, and brand identity systems for landscape and urban design projects. Specializes in editorial typography and data visualization for design narratives.

pixelChen.name: Pixel.Chen
pixelChen.bio: Creates photorealistic renderings, aerial perspectives, and immersive walkthrough animations. Proficient in Lumion, V-Ray, and real-time Unreal Engine environments for landscape visualization.

synthWong.name: Synth.Wong
synthWong.bio: Analyzes urban morphology, transit-oriented development patterns, and public realm strategies. Generates zoning studies, pedestrian flow models, and streetscape design frameworks for municipal-scale projects.

atlasFang.name: Atlas.Fang
atlasFang.bio: Synthesizes concept narratives and design intent across project phases. Oversees design coherence from schematic through construction documentation, ensuring alignment between vision and buildability.

0.headline: Playbook for the Pyrocene: Wildfire-Adaptive Landscape Design
0.teaser: A comprehensive guide offering six rules-of-thumb and 20 applied strategies for designing landscapes that adapt to increasingly frequent wildfire events.

1.headline: Carbon Conscience: Assessing Carbon Impact in Early Design Phases
1.teaser: A free web-based carbon calculator enabling designers to estimate emissions, storage and sequestration at the earliest stages of planning — when flexibility is highest.

2.headline: The Ellinikon Park: Practice-Based Research for Europe's Largest Coastal Park
2.teaser: Rigorous research into thermal comfort, carbon neutrality within 35 years, and ecological restoration using 3.3 million Greek-sourced native plants.

3.headline: Post-COVID Adaptive Planning: Rethinking the Urban Core
3.teaser: Analyzing shifts in pedestrian flow and public space utilization to create adaptive planning frameworks for dynamic, resilient urban centers.

4.headline: Decarbonizing the Design Process: A Phase-by-Phase Approach
4.teaser: A practical toolkit for landscape architects to reduce carbon emissions across all project phases — from kickoff through construction and operations.

5.headline: Nature Risk Screening: Piloting TNFD's LEAP Approach for Infrastructure
5.teaser: Applying the TNFD Locate-Evaluate-Assess-Prepare framework to infrastructure projects across Asia, shifting from reactive mitigation to strategic nature-positive planning.

6.headline: Invasive Species: Networks, Gadgets, and Commons in the Digitized Landscape
6.teaser: Investigating how surveillance cameras, location-based apps, and autonomous technologies are fundamentally altering the public realm and the designer's role.

7.headline: Climate Infrastructure Toolkit: Resilience Strategies for Urban Landscapes
7.teaser: A practical framework for integrating climate adaptation into landscape architecture, with case studies on coastal resilience, urban heat mitigation, and stormwater management.
